revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:赤崎神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Akasaki Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Akasaki-jinja, Toba 02.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Toba, Mie|en|Toba, Mie|qid=Q819629|lt=Toba, Mie|1=Toba, Mie}}, Toba 4-chome |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 28 |Latitude Seconds = 17.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 50 |Longitude Seconds = 44.5 |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Arasaki-hime no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |Established = |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Other Names = Akasaki-san |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = June 22 ({{ill|Akasaki Festival|ja|赤崎祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤崎神社|1=Akasaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635561}}) |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} Akasaki Procession (October 22) |location = {{ill|Toba|qid=Q69711656|1=Toba}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.4715|N|136.845694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Akasaki Shrine''' (赤崎神社, *Akasaki-jinja*) is a subordinate shrine (末社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Among the 125 shrines that make up Ise Grand Shrine, it is the only one located in {{ill|Toba, Mie|en|Toba, Mie|qid=Q819629|lt=Toba, Mie|1=Toba, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web|publisher=[[Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]] |url=http://ise.jr-central.co.jp/125/ |title=Discover and Experience Ise: The 125 Shrines of Ise |website=Visit Ise-Shima|accessdate=2011-05-10|qq=}}</ref> Another facility related to Ise Grand Shrine in Toba City is the {{ill|Jingū Goryō Awabi Processing Facility|ja|神宮御料鰒調製所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Goryō Awabi Processing Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in {{ill|Kuzaki, Toba|ja|国崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzaki, Toba|1=Kuzakichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11146786}}.<ref>{{Cite web|publisher={{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q687168}}|url=http://www.isejingu.or.jp/ryouti/ryouti5.htm |title=Awabi (Abalone) Processing Facility |website=Ise Grand Shrine|accessdate=2011-05-10|qq=}}</ref> Locally, it is affectionately known as "Akasaki-san" and revered as a {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} (guardian deity of one's birthplace).<ref name="ib">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 90.</ref> == Overview == Located on the slope of a mountain cliff near the mouth of the {{ill|Kamo River (Toba)|ja|加茂川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo River (Toba)|1=加茂川|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16279097}}, which flows north to south through {{ill|Toba, Mie|en|Toba, Mie|qid=Q819629|lt=Toba, Mie|1=Toba, Mie}} and empties into {{ill|Toba Bay|ja|鳥羽湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥羽港|1=Port of Toba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675103}}.<ref name="th0"/> Passing through a {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}} along {{ill|Japan National Route 167|en|Japan National Route 167|qid=Q367033|lt=National Route 167 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 167}} and climbing the {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone steps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 階段|1=Stairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q12511}} leads to the main shrine. Historical records such as the ''Jingū Ryakki'' and ''Shiyō Ryakushi'' mention that the shrine has been located at its current site.<ref name="th0"/> The shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style with a {{ill|board roof|ja|板葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roof|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=Wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q199388}} and is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fence.<ref name="th1">Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): p. 961</ref> To the left of the shrine grounds is an open area,<ref name="ib"/> believed to be the site of the previous shrine building.<ref name="ib"/> == Enshrined deity == The enshrined deity is Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto (荒崎姫命).<ref name="ib"/> She is regarded as the guardian deity of marine offerings (御贄, minie) collected from the {{ill|Toba Bay|ja|鳥羽湾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toba Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥羽港|1=Port of Toba|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675103}} coastline and dedicated to the Outer Shrine of Ise.<ref name="ib"/> Arasaki-hime-no-Mikoto, enshrined at {{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神前神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Arasaki Shrine|ja|荒前神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arasaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神前神社 (伊勢市)|1=Kōzaki Shrine & Komori Shrine & Arasaki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17228423}}, is the same deity worshiped at Akasaki Shrine. She is said to be a daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the younger sister of {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}.<ref>Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 80</ref> Records from the {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|qid=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū era|1=Kenkyū}} mention the ''Festival of Marine Offerings'' held on the 15th day of the 6th month, where deities such as Akushi, Akasaki, and Kaburako Myōjin were worshiped.<ref name="th0">Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): p. 960</ref> == History == The ''History of Toba City, Volume 2'' contains no specific details about the founding of Akasaki Shrine. The current location of the shrine falls within the former territory of {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|qid=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}. There are no historical records suggesting a direct connection to shrines under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} in [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. One theory suggests that the original site of Akasaki Shrine was {{ill|Kata Shrine|ja|賀多神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kata Shrine|1=Kata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634883}}, located north of the Myōkeigawa River, which marked the border between Ise and Shima Provinces.<ref>Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): pp. 960–961</ref> While currently situated on a cliffside, it is believed that the shrine was once located along the coast.<ref name="th0"/> During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, there was a {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|qid=Q94498|lt=kagura hall|1=Kagura-den}}, but it no longer exists.<ref name="th0"/> == Akasaki Festival == [[File:Akasaki matsuri.jpg|thumb|Lanterns during the Akasaki Festival]] The '''Akasaki Festival''' (赤崎祭, Akasaki Matsuri) is held annually on June 22 at Akasaki Shrine. It is often said that the festival day tends to be rainy,<ref name="tn">{{Cite web|publisher=Toba City Tourism Division|url=http://www.city.toba.mie.jp/cgi-bin/kanko/diarypro301/diary.cgi|title=Toba Biyori / Akasaki Festival|date=2010-06-23|accessdate=2011-05-08|qq=}}</ref> but it remains a signature event heralding the arrival of summer in Toba.<ref name="tta">{{Cite web|publisher=Toba City Tourism Association|url=http://www.toba.gr.jp/200/post_560.html|title=Akasaki Festival|accessdate=2011-05-08|qq=}}</ref> The festival attracts many visitors from Toba and surrounding areas, including offshore {{ill|Island|en|Island|qid=Q23442|lt=islands|1=Island}} and {{ill|Isobe, Shima|ja|志摩市磯部町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe, Shima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="th1"/> In 2010 (Heisei 22), around 8,000 people attended.<ref name="tn"/> Known as the ''Yukata Festival,'' people of all ages wearing yukata gather to enjoy the festivities.<ref name="tta"/> Stalls line the street from Akasaki Shrine to {{ill|Nakanogō Station|en|Nakanogō Station|qid=Q6960279|lt=Nakano-go Station|1=Nakanogō Station}} on the {{ill|Shima Line|en|Shima Line|qid=Q5357604|lt=Kintetsu Shima Line|1=Shima Line}},<ref name="tta"/> with over 100 vendors.<ref name="ib"/> Since the 2004 festival, a tofu shop founded in 1916, Kōjiya, has sold ''soy milk soft-serve ice cream,'' becoming especially popular among women and children, with the demand reportedly causing hand strain for shop staff.<ref>{{Cite news|publisher={{ill|Ise-Shima Economic Newspaper|ja|伊勢志摩経済新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Shima Economic Newspaper|1=ISESHIMA KEIZAI SHIMBUN|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18700592}}|url=http://iseshima.keizai.biz/headline/205/|title=Tofu Shop’s Soy Milk Soft-Serve Ice Cream Popular at Yukata Festival|date=2007-06-19|qq=}}</ref> === Festival Customs === On the festival day, monthly {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} rituals are held within the shrine grounds. The shrine’s reverence association sells small cedar branches collected from the shrine precinct at the entrance.<ref name="tn"/><ref>{{Cite web|publisher=Toba City Tourism Division|url=http://tobakanko.jp/modules/event/index.php?p=13|website=Toba City Tourism Information Site|title=Event / Akasaki Festival|accessdate=2011-05-08|qq=}}</ref> This practice began due to the high demand for these sacred branches.<ref name="th1"/> A local belief holds that homes displaying these branches avoided illness during epidemics, leading to the custom of hanging them at household entrances.<ref name="tn"/> It is thought to be a variant of {{ill|Somin Shorai faith|ja|蘇民信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Somin Shorai faith|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="th1"/> == Access == ; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shima-Akasaki Station|en|Shima-Akasaki Station|qid=Q1447107|lt=Shima-Akasaki Station|1=Shima-Akasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Shima Line|en|Shima Line|qid=Q5357604|lt=Kintetsu Shima Line|1=Shima Line}}. * Get off at ''Akasaki Shrine-mae'' bus stop on the {{ill|Kamome Bus|ja|かもめバス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamome Bus|1=Kamome Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11264268}} community transportation system. ; By Car * Located along {{ill|Japan National Route 167|en|Japan National Route 167|qid=Q367033|lt=National Route 167 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 167}}. From {{ill|Toba Station|en|Toba Station|qid=Q7811445|lt=Toba Station|1=Toba Station}}, head south on the national road, turn right at the Anagashima Bridge North intersection, and continue a short distance to see the torii of Akasaki Shrine on the right. Note that there is no nearby parking available. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 鳥羽市史編さん室 編『鳥羽市史 下巻』鳥羽市役所、平成3rd year 3月25日、1347p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == External Links == * [https://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m11/1101.html 赤崎神社（あかさきじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://tobakanko.jp/modules/know/index.php?p=32 赤崎神社] - 鳥羽市観光情報サイト（鳥羽市観光課） * [http://www.city.toba.mie.jp/kankou-kikaku/event/akasaki.html 赤崎祭り] - 鳥羽市公式サイト * [http://www.kirari1000.com/www.kirari1000.com.base_data.base_data.phpQkirari_cd=03662.html 赤崎神社とゆかた祭り] - [[伊勢志摩きらり千選]]（財団法人伊勢志摩国立公園協会） {{Normdaten|qq=}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|赤崎神社|version=103865270|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:赤崎神社]]
